Matt Vonderheide 04/02/2020 1.0 Updated to include null LM end time at pack completion Kelly Faria 08/19/2020 2.0 Updated LM Performance screen display Kelly Faria 08/26/2020 2.1 Added clarification to LM DB fields being referenced EX 13 – Custom Task Auto Complete UI McLane divisions perform picking in repack and single-sell areas using pick sheets . McLane requires that the labor is tracked for this picking process and McLane does not use RF device for this picking process . In addition to that McLane requires t he user to be able to view the performance of the last task and performance for the day. To fulfil this requirement, a custom UI is developed in WMOS to accept user id and picking task. WMOS packs all task details for the task. This results in inaccurate labor tracking on time spent by the user. To resolve this, additional changes are made in both LM and WMOS. In WMOS, end time for the labor message is nullified so that LM can track labor based on heel-to-toe metrics . After loading and auto-completing the task, a custom screen displays the user performance. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Custom Task Auto Complete UI and Custom User Performance UI MHE N RF Y RF Pack Cubed Directed – Null Task End Time in LM Message Reporting N 2` Other N A new custom UI , Task Auto Complete UI , is developed to accept the user id and pick task. User can key enter the user id or scan the badge using scanner connected to the terminal. User can key enter the task or scan the task barcode from pick sheet using scanner connected to the terminal. WMOS performs validation on the user and task. If user or task fail custom validation, WMOS displays corresponding custom error messages. Once validations pass, WMOS calls RF Pack Cubed Directed program setup through custom configuration . The RF Pack Cubed Directed program auto-completes the pick task using scanned user id and nullifies the task end time . WMOS then loads the LM Performance Metrics UI to display use r’s performance for the last task and user’s performance for the day. User clicks the Exit action in the UI and WMOS loads the Task Auto Complete UI for scanning user id and task. Assumptions Custom Task Auto Complete UI is only utilized for Picking tasks using pick sheets . A dedicated user credential is used to login to WMOS UI. This is needed to keep the WMOS UI session running and with custom Task Auto Complete UI open . This dedicated user is configur ed with same permission as the picker . The WM OS /LM application is logged in with a generic kiosk user ID. Each user scanning Label Picking tasks uses the custom UI individually. RF Pack Cubed Directed program linked to the custom Task Auto Complete UI is configured to auto-complete the pick task scanned by the user. Users can only scan 1 task at a time. The P p ick task s are c reated is created with wave per McLane requirement ; s ingle task per per McLane requirement , to pick all oLPNs within warehouse area/aisle per order destination for the order destination using the single task . A user id is configured with required permissions in WMOS for the user badge number . Base Activity Tracking for RF Pack Cubed Directed is not modified as part of this extension. McLane uses LM Heel-to-toe setting of ‘Accept Begin times’ in Additional Facility Configuration . Base Labor Management Performance calculations and reporting are not modified as part of this extension. The custom LM Performance Metrics UI can be accessed only through custom Task Auto Complete UI after scanning the user id and pick task. Users have a valid clock in available for the warehouse day. Users have one unique clock in record per warehouse day (E_EMP_PERF_SMRY record). “ Previous task” is defined as the last tasked event performed by a user within their CICO record prior to the current task scanned. “Current day” is defined as all processed events associated to the current CICO record for the user . “Next task” is defined as the task scanned by the user for completion before loading Performance Metrics screen. Actual time for previous task is assumed based on (sysdate – event start time) as it is not yet calculated at the time the Performance metrics are displayed to the user . EP% for current day is l atest performance calculated by LM batch job that runs every X minutes plus previous task EP% . If multiple tasks are scanned by the same user in quick succession, recent tasks may not have been processed by the LM batch and therefore are not included in the EP%. LABOR_TRAN.RELEASED_WORK referenced by Goal times and EP% uses a flat PF&D rate as configured in Additional Facility Configuration. LABOR_TRAN.RELEASED_WORK referenced by Goal times and EP% do not take Training Curves or Performance Goals into account . Calculated ti me is available prior to the time that the user begin s work on a task . The SLA for the availability of calculated time after the wave runs will be determined at a later time. All relevant Labor Monitoring batches and schedulers are configured, and Labor Monitoring is turned on. The labor monitoring matching between LABOR_TRAN and LABOR_MSG is done based on TASK_ NBR as per base. McLane configures RF Pack Cubed Directed with verification mode ‘6 – No Verification Required’ and entry mod e of ‘1 – oLPN’ to support the operational flow related to this extension. If the program is configured to use a different screen flow, the LM end time logic of this extension still occurs and may result in inaccurate metrics. This extension does not capture actual travel time or time spent in each location. Packing updates by RF Pack Cubed Directed complete s all details of the task. No exceptions/shorts are supported in this process. Any chase must be performed manually. Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement Glossary Manhattan Associates Terminology McLane Terminology Description Goal Time Estimated time for completion of a task based on task details and configured labor standards . Actual Time Real time taken to complete a task based on event start and end times. EP% Employee Performance % - goal time / actual time (taking into account breaks, break overlaps, etc) including all Job Functions for that CICO record P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow Process Steps: WMOS prompts for the user id and pick task in custom Task Auto Complete UI. User enters/ scans badge number and task id from pick sheet. WMOS validates the scanned user id and task. If validation fails, WMOS displays custom error message . With successful validation, WMOS auto-completes the pick task , generates the labor message and nullifies the event end time. After task updates, WMOS displays custom LM Performance Metrics UI. User Exits LM Performance Metrics UI and WMOS load s Task Auto Complete UI UI Enablement IF C-00 0 -000 byte 1 = Y  (global extension enablement)   IF C-00 0 -E X 13 byte 1 = Y  (extension specific enablement)   THEN the functionality detailed in this section is enabled   ELSE the functionality detailed in the section is disabled   ELSE the functionality detailed in the section is disabled   ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled   Custom Permission A custom permission is developed to control access to th e Task Auto Complete UI – Task Auto Complete – View . Screen 1 – Task Auto Complete UI A custom UI is developed to allow users to scan and complete Label Picking tasks. Display # Literal Source Display Length (R/L justified) 1 ‘ User Id ’ N/A 20A (L) 2 ‘Pick Task’ N/A 20A (L) User Entry # Field Description Display Length (R/L justified) 3 Textbox - User Id Cursor focus by default. User scans the badge . User can also key enter the user id. 20A (R) 4 Textbox - Pick Task User scan the task from User . User can also key enter the task . 20A (R) User Actions Load Task Validations: WMOS fi r st validates if the extension is enabled. If extension is disabled , display custom error message CUST/1301 “EX13 is disabled, contact administrator” . Otherwise , WMOS proceeds with custom validations. WMOS validates if RF Pack Cubed Directed program configured in the custom Task Auto Complete UI transaction parameter 1-25 . If the bytes 1 -25 are blank or not configured with RF Pack Cubed Directed program base parameter verification mode ( byte 4-4 ) as “No verification required” and entry mode ( byte 30-30 ) as “ oLPN ” , WMOS displays custom error message CUST/130 2 - “Invalid packing program configured, contact administrator”. After validating RF program, WMOS performs base validations for the scanner user id. If the scanned user id does not exist in WMOS , WMOS display s base error message for user does not exist . If the scanned user id exist s but does not have custom permission mentioned in section 4.2 to perform custom auto-complete process, WMOS displays custom error message CUST/130 3 - “Insufficient permission for user: {0} ” where parameter 0 is the scanned user id . Once the user id is successfully validated, WMOS validates the pick task. If scanned task is not found as INT50 task with status between released and assigned , WMOS displays custom error message CUST/130 4 - “Invalid pick task: {0} ” where parameter 0 is the scanned pick task. Once pick task successfully validated , WMOS loads RF Pack Cubed Directed program configured in the Task Auto Complete UI transaction parameter bytes 1 -25 to auto-complete the pick task Updates: RF Pack Cubed Directed program covered in RF section performs packing updates . Navigation: Upon validation failure, return to Task Auto Complete UI and display the error message. Cursor Focus : Upon validation failure, return the focus to User id text box After successful task auto-complete in RF Pack Cubed Directed, WMOS loads the custom LM Performance Metrics UI. Reset User clicks the Reset action to reset the values in the form. No call to the server for this action. Validations: N/A . Updates: N/A . Navigation: N/A Cursor Focus: Return the focus to User id text box Outputs Activity Tracking No custom activity tracking. RF Pack Cubed Directed tracks activity. PIX Not applicable. LM Hook RF section covers the labor tracking by RF Pack Cubed Directed . Screen 2 – LM Performance Metrics Once the user loads a task to begin work, the UI screen updates to display the following LM performance metrics to the user. Display Validations: WMOS performs the following validations before displaying LM Performance Metrics screen: User has a valid Employee Status record If the user does not have a valid Employee Status record in E_EMP_DTL, then do not load LM Performance Metrics and instead display custom error message CUST/130 5 - “User {0} is missing Employee Status Record” , where parameter 0 is the scanned user id. User has a valid Clock In record for warehouse day If the user does not have a valid Clock In record in E_EMP_PERF_SMRY for the warehouse day , then do not load LM Pe rformance Metrics and instead display custom error message CUST/130 6 - “ User {0} is not clocked in ” , where parameter 0 is the scanned user id. Task is user’s first task of the day If the user does not have any previous tasks between the current sysdate and their clock in time (E_EMP_PERF_SMRY.CLOCK_IN_DATE) , o nly load Goal for Next Task values , do not display metrics for Previous Task or Current Day. If all validations pass, display the following fields. Performance – Previous Task Previous task refers to the last tasked event performed by a user within their CICO record prior to the current task scanned . Tasked event is determined by a populated TASK_NBR field on the labor message record . EP % (Previous Task) uses the following formula : EP % ( Previous Task ) = ( Goal Time (Previous Task) as calculated in section 4.4.1.2 ) / ( Actual Time (Previous Task) as calculated in section 4.4.1.3 ) * 100 EP % (Previous Task) is rounded down to the nearest whole percentage . Ex. If EP% = 109.876, round down to 109% EP % (Previous Task) only considers the last tasked event for that user. It does not consider non-tasked events when determining for user’s previous task. Goal Time – Previous Task Goal Time for the previous task is pulled from LABOR_TRAN which was populated prior to this processing by AsyncWMOSMonitorEventProcessingBatch . The task is matched to a LABOR_TRAN record by the TASK_NBR. Goal Time (Previous Task) = LABOR_TRAN.RELEASED_WORK Goal Time (Previous Task) is displayed in the format ‘X Min X Secs ’ rounded to the nearest second. For example, 4.753 minutes would display a s ‘4 Min 45 seconds ’ ’ Actual Time – Previous Task Actual T ime for the previous task is inferred based on the system time – labor message start time . This is because the end time for the labor event is not yet calculated at the time the Performance metrics are displayed to the user. The task is matched to a LABOR_MSG record by the TASK_NBR and LOGIN_USER_ID. Actual Time (Previous Task) = Sysdate – LABOR_MSG.SCHED_START_DATE Actual Time (Previous Task) is displayed in the format ‘X Min X Secs’ rounded to the nearest second. For example, 4.753 minutes would display a s ‘4 Min 45 seconds’ Performance – Current Day Current Day refers to all events from the user’s Clock In to the current time (as of last batch process). EP % (Current Day) uses the following formula: EP % (Current Day) = ( Goal Time (Current Day) as calculated in section 4.4.1.5 ) / ( Actual Time (Current Day) as calculated in section 4.4.1.6 ) * 100 EP % ( Current Day ) is updated as of the last batch process run plus the previous task values. EP % (Current Day) is rounded down to the nearest whole percentage . Ex. If EP% = 109.876, round down to 109% LABOR_TRAN.RELEASED_WORK is store d in the DB as minutes Goal Time – Current Day Goal Time is determined using the configurable formula for EP numerator in Additional Facility Configuration . This value is populated in TOTAL_PAM in the user’s E_EMP_PERF_SMRY record by the ReprocessCICO batch. Goal Time (Current Day) references this value and makes the following adjustments to include the previous task in this calculation: Goal Time (Current Day) = E_EMP_PERF_SMRY.TOTAL_PAM + Goal Time (Previous Task) as calculated in section 4.4.1.2 Goal Time (Current Day) is updated as of the last batch process run plus the previous task values. Goal Time (Current Day) is displayed in the format ‘X Hr X Min’ if greater than 1 hour, else ‘X Min X Secs’ rounded to the nearest minute or second respectively . For example, 4.753 minutes would display a s ‘4 Min 45 seconds’ E_EMP_PERF_SMRY.TOTAL_PAM is stored in the DB as minutes Actual Time – Current Day Actual Time is determined using the configurable formula for EP denominator in Additional Facility Configuration . This value is populated TOTAL_NONSTDLABOR_TIME in the user’s E_EMP_PERF_SMRY record by the ReprocessCICO batch. Actual Time (Current Day) references this value and makes the following adjustments to include the previous task in this calculation: Actual Time (Current Day) = E_EMP_PERF_SMRY. TOTAL_NONSTDLABOR_TIME + Actual Time (Previous Task) as calculated in section 4.4.1. 3 Actual Time (Current Day) is updated as of the last batch process run plus the previous task values. Actual Time (Current Day) is displayed in the format ‘X Hr X Min’ if greater than 1 hour, else ‘X Min X Secs’ rounded to the nearest minute or second respectively. For example, 4.753 minutes would display as ‘4 Min 45 seconds’ E_EMP_PERF_SMRY.TOTAL_NONSTDLABOR_TIME is stored in the DB as minutes Goal Time – Next Task Next task refers to the task scanned by the user for completion before loading the Performance Metrics screen. Goal Time for the next task is pulled from LABOR_TRAN which was populated prior to this processing by AsyncWMOSMonitorEventProcessingBatch . The task is matched to a LABOR_TRAN record by the TASK_NBR. Goal Time ( Next Task) = LABOR_TRAN.RELEASED_WORK Goal Time ( Next Task) is displayed in the format ‘X Min X Secs’ rounded to the nearest second. For example, 4.753 minutes would display as ‘4 Min 45 seconds’ LABOR_TRAN.RELEASED_WORK is stored in the DB as minutes User Actions Exit Validations: N/A. Updates: N/A. Navigation: WMOS l oads the Task Auto Complete UI. Cursor Focus: Return the focus to User id text box . Screen Timeout If user does not select ‘Exit’ to return to the Task Auto Complete UI, WMOS refers to the Task Auto Complete UI transaction parameter 26-27 to determine UI timeout time (in seconds). Once this time has elapsed, WMOS loads the Task Auto Complete UI. Cursor Focus: Return the focus to User id text box. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF RF Pack Cubed Dir - Null Task End Date/Time WM OS is modified by the logic of this section to generate LM records with a null end date/time and performs packing updates using scanned user id . The actual end time of the previous event is calculated using heel-to-toe logic when the next task is begun by scanning another pick task . Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX1 3 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX1 3 is turned off, and no custom EX1 3 processing will occur. Lastly, the custom RF transaction parameter must be enabled to nullify the end time of the labor message . This is configured in RF Pack Cubed Directed transaction parameter 504-504. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX1 3 is turned off, and no custom EX1 3 processing will occur. Validations There are no additional, custom validations as part of this extension. Updates Use scanned user id for packing updates. Then perform custom updates covered in LM hooks section as below. Outputs Activity Tracking Not in scope for this extension. PIX Not in scope for this extension. LM Hooks The LM Hook to calculate actual end time of a task at task completion is modified by this extension to communicate null values. LM then calculates the end time of the task based on the start time of the next task using heel-to-toe logic. Hook Enablement The following section describes the configuration required to enable the LM hook for the transaction. If the LM Hook is disabled, no LM events are generated and the logic of this extension does not occur. T he LM Interface is enabled, if the following conditions are true: WHSE_PARAMETERS.ELS_INSTL = 1 TRAN_MASTER.ELS_ACTVTY_CODE <> NULL (for the transaction) TRAN_MASTER.WORKINFO_INTERFACE = 1 or 2 ( Interface will always be set to 1 for th is transaction) Generate 005 – Header Message There are no changes the LM Header message as part of this extension. Matt Vonderheide 04/02/2020 1.0 Updated to include null LM end time at pack completion Kelly Faria 08/19/2020 2.0 Updated LM Performance screen display Kelly Faria 08/26/2020 2.1 Added clarification to LM DB fields being referenced EX 13 – Custom Task Auto Complete UI McLane divisions perform picking in repack and single-sell areas using pick sheets . McLane requires that the labor is tracked for this picking process and McLane does not use RF device for this picking process . In addition to that McLane requires t he user to be able to view the performance of the last task and performance for the day. To fulfil this requirement, a custom UI is developed in WMOS to accept user id and picking task. WMOS packs all task details for the task. This results in inaccurate labor tracking on time spent by the user. To resolve this, additional changes are made in both LM and WMOS. In WMOS, end time for the labor message is nullified so that LM can track labor based on heel-to-toe metrics . After loading and auto-completing the task, a custom screen displays the user performance. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Custom Task Auto Complete UI and Custom User Performance UI MHE N RF Y RF Pack Cubed Directed – Null Task End Time in LM Message Reporting N 2` Other N A new custom UI , Task Auto Complete UI , is developed to accept the user id and pick task.

Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Custom Task Auto Complete UI and Custom User Performance UI MHE N RF Y RF Pack Cubed Directed – Null Task End Time in LM Message Reporting N 2` Other N A new custom UI , Task Auto Complete UI , is developed to accept the user id and pick task.

User can key enter the user id or scan the badge using scanner connected to the terminal. User can key enter the task or scan the task barcode from pick sheet using scanner connected to the terminal. WMOS performs validation on the user and task. If user or task fail custom validation, WMOS displays corresponding custom error messages. Once validations pass, WMOS calls RF Pack Cubed Directed program setup through custom configuration . The RF Pack Cubed Directed program auto-completes the pick task using scanned user id and nullifies the task end time . WMOS then loads the LM Performance Metrics UI to display use r’s performance for the last task and user’s performance for the day. User clicks the Exit action in the UI and WMOS loads the Task Auto Complete UI for scanning user id and task. Assumptions Custom Task Auto Complete UI is only utilized for Picking tasks using pick sheets . A dedicated user credential is used to login to WMOS UI. This is needed to keep the WMOS UI session running and with custom Task Auto Complete UI open . This dedicated user is configur ed with same permission as the picker . The WM OS /LM application is logged in with a generic kiosk user ID. Each user scanning Label Picking tasks uses the custom UI individually. RF Pack Cubed Directed program linked to the custom Task Auto Complete UI is configured to auto-complete the pick task scanned by the user. Users can only scan 1 task at a time. The P p ick task s are c reated is created with wave per McLane requirement ; s ingle task per per McLane requirement , to pick all oLPNs within warehouse area/aisle per order destination for the order destination using the single task . A user id is configured with required permissions in WMOS for the user badge number . Base Activity Tracking for RF Pack Cubed Directed is not modified as part of this extension. McLane uses LM Heel-to-toe setting of ‘Accept Begin times’ in Additional Facility Configuration . Base Labor Management Performance calculations and reporting are not modified as part of this extension. The custom LM Performance Metrics UI can be accessed only through custom Task Auto Complete UI after scanning the user id and pick task. Users have a valid clock in available for the warehouse day. Users have one unique clock in record per warehouse day (E_EMP_PERF_SMRY record). “ Previous task” is defined as the last tasked event performed by a user within their CICO record prior to the current task scanned.

[Document: MCLN EX13 Custom Task Auto Complete UI]

user id and pick task. Users have a valid clock in available for the warehouse day. Users have one unique clock in record per warehouse day (E_EMP_PERF_SMRY record). “ Previous task” is defined as the last tasked event performed by a user within their CICO record prior to the current task scanned.

“Current day” is defined as all processed events associated to the current CICO record for the user . “Next task” is defined as the task scanned by the user for completion before loading Performance Metrics screen. Actual time for previous task is assumed based on (sysdate – event start time) as it is not yet calculated at the time the Performance metrics are displayed to the user . EP% for current day is l atest performance calculated by LM batch job that runs every X minutes plus previous task EP% . If multiple tasks are scanned by the same user in quick succession, recent tasks may not have been processed by the LM batch and therefore are not included in the EP%. LABOR_TRAN.RELEASED_WORK referenced by Goal times and EP% uses a flat PF&D rate as configured in Additional Facility Configuration. LABOR_TRAN.RELEASED_WORK referenced by Goal times and EP% do not take Training Curves or Performance Goals into account . Calculated ti me is available prior to the time that the user begin s work on a task . The SLA for the availability of calculated time after the wave runs will be determined at a later time. All relevant Labor Monitoring batches and schedulers are configured, and Labor Monitoring is turned on. The labor monitoring matching between LABOR_TRAN and LABOR_MSG is done based on TASK_ NBR as per base. McLane configures RF Pack Cubed Directed with verification mode ‘6 – No Verification Required’ and entry mod e of ‘1 – oLPN’ to support the operational flow related to this extension. If the program is configured to use a different screen flow, the LM end time logic of this extension still occurs and may result in inaccurate metrics. This extension does not capture actual travel time or time spent in each location. Packing updates by RF Pack Cubed Directed complete s all details of the task. No exceptions/shorts are supported in this process. Any chase must be performed manually. is enabled. If extension is disabled , display custom error message CUST/1301 “EX13 is disabled, contact administrator” . Otherwise , WMOS proceeds with custom validations. WMOS validates if RF Pack Cubed Directed program configured in the custom Task Auto Complete UI transaction parameter 1-25 .

If the bytes 1 -25 are blank or not configured with RF Pack Cubed Directed program base parameter verification mode ( byte 4-4 ) as “No verification required” and entry mode ( byte 30-30 ) as “ oLPN ” , WMOS displays custom error message CUST/130 2 - “Invalid packing program configured, contact administrator”. After validating RF program, WMOS performs base validations for the scanner user id. If the scanned user id does not exist in WMOS , WMOS display s base error message for user does not exist . If the scanned user id exist s but does not have custom permission mentioned in section 4.2 to perform custom auto-complete process, WMOS displays custom error message CUST/130 3 - “Insufficient permission for user: {0} ” where parameter 0 is the scanned user id . Once the user id is successfully validated, WMOS validates the pick task. cking. RF Pack Cubed Directed tracks activity. PIX Not applicable. LM Hook RF section covers the labor tracking by RF Pack Cubed Directed . Screen 2 – LM Performance Metrics Once the user loads a task to begin work, the UI screen updates to display the following LM performance metrics to the user.

Display Validations: WMOS performs the following validations before displaying LM Performance Metrics screen: User has a valid Employee Status record If the user does not have a valid Employee Status record in E_EMP_DTL, then do not load LM Performance Metrics and instead display custom error message CUST/130 5 - “User {0} is missing Employee Status Record” , where parameter 0 is the scanned user id. User has a valid Clock In record for warehouse day If the user does not have a valid Clock In record in E_EMP_PERF_SMRY for the warehouse day , then do not load LM Pe rformance Metrics and instead display custom error message CUST/130 6 - “ User {0} is not clocked in ” , where parameter 0 is the scanned user id. Task is user’s first task of the day If the user does not have any previous tasks between the current sysdate and their clock in time (E_EMP_PERF_SMRY.CLOCK_IN_DATE) , o nly load Goal for Next Task values , do not display metrics for Previous Task or Current Day. If all validations pass, display the following fields. Performance – Previous Task Previous task refers to the last tasked event performed by a user within their CICO record prior to the current task scanned . Tasked event is determined by a populated TASK_NBR field on the labor message record . EP % (Previous Task) uses the following formula : EP % ( Previous Task ) = ( Goal Time (Previous Task) as calculated in section 4.4.1.2 ) / ( Actual Time (Previous Task) as calculated in section 4.4.1.3 ) * 100 EP % (Previous Task) is rounded down to the nearest whole percentage . Ex. If EP% = 109.876, round down to 109% EP % (Previous Task) only considers the last tasked event for that user. It does not consider non-tasked events when determining for user’s previous task. Goal Time – Previous Task Goal Time for the previous task is pulled from LABOR_TRAN which was populated prior to this processing by AsyncWMOSMonitorEventProcessingBatch . The task is matched to a LABOR_TRAN record by the TASK_NBR. Goal Time (Previous Task) = LABOR_TRAN.RELEASED_WORK Goal Time (Previous Task) is displayed in the format ‘X Min X Secs ’ rounded to the nearest second. RF RF Pack Cubed Dir - Null Task End Date/Time WM OS is modified by the logic of this section to generate LM records with a null end date/time and performs packing updates using scanned user id . The actual end time of the previous event is calculated using heel-to-toe logic when the next task is begun by scanning another pick task . Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX1 3 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag.

If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX1 3 is turned off, and no custom EX1 3 processing will occur. Lastly, the custom RF transaction parameter must be enabled to nullify the end time of the labor message . This is configured in RF Pack Cubed Directed transaction parameter 504-504. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX1 3 is turned off, and no custom EX1 3 processing will occur. LM Hooks The LM Hook to calculate actual end time of a task at task completion is modified by this extension to communicate null values. LM then calculates the end time of the task based on the start time of the next task using heel-to-toe logic. Hook Enablement The following section describes the configuration required to enable the LM hook for the transaction. If the LM Hook is disabled, no LM events are generated and the logic of this extension does not occur.
